title: Xtra Crisp Korean Mulukhiyah Jeon
description: A traditional Korean dish from the Chungcheong region, Mulukhiyah Jeon is a crispy and cozy jeon featuring mulukhiyah, cooked to perfection in a clay pot, this gluten-free friendly recipe is sure to delight. It has a distinct bitter flavor balanced by the crunch of the jeon.
instructions: In a large bowl, whisk together rice flour, cornstarch, salt, and black pepper.
Add the eggs to the bowl and mix until a smooth batter forms.
Stir in the minced garlic and sesame oil.
Gradually add the mulukhiyah leaves to the batter and mix until they are evenly coated.
Heat the vegetable oil in a clay pot over medium heat.
Using a spoon, drop small amounts of the mulukhiyah batter into the oil and flatten them slightly.
Cook the jeon for 3-4 minutes on each side, until they are crispy and golden brown.
Remove the jeon from the oil and place them on a paper towel-lined plate to drain excess oil.
Serve the Xtra Crisp Korean Mulukhiyah Jeon hot and enjoy.
